paper.title: MING: An Automated CNN-to-Edge MLIR HLS framework
paper.abstract: Driven by the increasing demand for low-latency and real-time processing, machine learning applications are steadily migrating toward edge computing platforms, where Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) are widely adopted for their energy efficiency compared to CPUs and GPUs. To generate high-performance and low-power FPGA designs, several frameworks built upon High Level Synthesis (HLS) vendor tools have been proposed, among which MLIR-based frameworks are gaining significant traction due to

memory_insight.before_after_comparisons: 15x speedup for standard CNN kernels with up to four layers, and up to 200x for single-layer kernels

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing a streaming architecture with carefully managed buffers can significantly improve performance and reduce resource constraints in edge devices.
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, this can be deployed on both mobile and laptop devices due to its efficient design and low resource requirements.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
